title: Dianthera dasyclados
taxonomy: Dianthera dasyclados belongs to the family Acanthaceae, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as the acanthus or thorny flowers. The genus Dianthera is a small group of plants native to the tropical regions of the Americas. Dianthera dasyclados is a type of flowering plant that is native to the tropical and subtropical regions of the Americas. It is classified as a perennial herb, which means it grows and flourishes for multiple years. The classification of Dianthera dasyclados is further supported by its unique characteristics, such as its distinctive flowers and leaves.
